title: suggestRelatedEdges pure function (token Jaccard + tag overlap + shared paths, confidence >= 0.6)
description: Add a pure, I/O-free `suggestRelatedEdges(nodes)` beside buildRelatedGraph in doctor-related-graph.ts that PROPOSES related edges the corpus is missing. Judgment uses existing RuleDescription fields (agents.ts:5-39 — note there is NO `keywords` field): tokenize() (CJK-friendly) over summary + intent_clues for Jaccard, tag-set intersection (`tags?` — a SEPARATE overlap signal, not folded into the token set), and shared relevance_paths. Only pairs scoring confidence >= 0.6 are proposed; each carries a provenance marker naming which signal(s) fired. No writes, no advisory wiring here — that is TASK-004.
action: In packages/server/src/services/doctor-related-graph.ts add an exported interface `SuggestedRelatedEdge { source: string; target: string; confidence: number; provenance: string[] }` and an exported pure `function suggestRelatedEdges(nodes: RelatedGraphNodeRich[]): SuggestedRelatedEdge[]`. Extend the node input to carry the fields needed (a new `RelatedGraphNodeRich` with qualifiedId, summary, intentClues[] (from RuleDescription.intent_clues — RuleDescription has NO `keywords` field, agents.ts:5-39), tags[], relevancePaths[], existing related[]). For every unordered pair not already connected via existing `related`, compute: jaccard = |A∩B|/|A∪B| over tokenize(summary+' '+intentClues.join(' ')) term sets; tagOverlap = |tagsA∩tagsB|>0 (tags kept as an INDEPENDENT signal, not merged into the token set); pathOverlap = |pathsA∩pathsB|>0. confidence = weighted combine (jaccard dominant, +bonus for tag/path overlap), clamp [0,1]. Push {source,target,confidence,provenance} only when confidence >= 0.6, provenance lists 'token-jaccard'/'tag-overlap'/'shared-path' for each firing signal. Deterministic ordering (confidence desc, then source,target). Import tokenize from @fenglimg/fabric-shared.

criteria: packages/server/src/services/doctor-related-graph.ts contains 'export function suggestRelatedEdges' and 'export interface SuggestedRelatedEdge'
suggestRelatedEdges imports tokenize from '@fenglimg/fabric-shared' (uses the CJK-aware tokenizer, not an ad-hoc split)

the token source is summary + intent_clues; RelatedGraphNodeRich uses `intentClues` and the file contains NO reference to a `keywords` description field (RuleDescription has none — guard against the tsc failure): grep of doctor-related-graph.ts for '.keywords' returns nothing
tags are used as an INDEPENDENT tag-overlap signal (not concatenated into the tokenize() input)
the function is PURE — no import of collectStoreCanonicalEntries / fs / disk inside suggestRelatedEdges (grep shows no fs or collectStore call in the function body)
a pair already connected via existing `related` is NEVER re-proposed (self-and-existing-edge exclusion, unit-tested)
only pairs with confidence >= 0.6 are returned; every returned edge has a non-empty provenance[] naming the firing signal(s)
returned edges are deterministically ordered (confidence desc, then source, then target) — same input yields same output
a unit test with hand-built fixtures asserts: high-token-overlap pair proposed, unrelated pair not proposed, existing-edge pair excluded
convergence.verification: node -e "const s=require('fs').readFileSync('packages/server/src/services/doctor-related-graph.ts','utf8'); process.exit(s.includes('export function suggestRelatedEdges')&&s.includes('SuggestedRelatedEdge')&&!/suggestRelatedEdges[\s\S]*?collectStoreCanonicalEntries/.test(s.slice(s.indexOf('function suggestRelatedEdges'), s.indexOf('function suggestRelatedEdges')+2000))?0:1)"; pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit; pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test -- doctor-related-graph
convergence.definition_of_done: Given a set of KB nodes, the system can compute which missing related edges are worth proposing (with a confidence and a why), as a deterministic pure function ready to be surfaced as an advisory.

files.change: Add the interface + rich node type + pure suggestRelatedEdges. Pairwise Jaccard over tokenize(summary+intent_clues) + tag intersection (independent signal) + shared relevance_paths; confidence >= 0.6 gate; provenance per firing signal; deterministic order. tokenize imported from @fenglimg/fabric-shared. RelatedGraphNodeRich uses intentClues[] (RuleDescription.intent_clues) — NOT keywords (no such field).

implementation: Read RuleDescription (agents.ts:5-39) to confirm the real text fields — summary (required), intent_clues[], tech_stack[], impact[], must_read_if, tags?[] — there is NO `keywords` field, so the token source is summary + intent_clues.

Read buildRelatedGraph (:70) and RelatedGraphNode (:49) to match the module's pure-function style; read StoreCanonicalEntry (cross-store-recall.ts:491) to see which description fields (summary, intent_clues, tags, relevance_paths, related) are available for TASK-004 to map in.
Read tokenize (text-tokenize.ts:64) to confirm the term-array contract (duplicates included → dedupe to a Set for Jaccard).
Define SuggestedRelatedEdge { source, target, confidence, provenance } and RelatedGraphNodeRich { qualifiedId, summary, intentClues, tags, relevancePaths, related }.
Build a Set of existing edges (both directions) from each node's related[] so connected pairs are skipped.

For each unordered pair: compute token Sets via tokenize(summary+' '+intentClues.join(' ')), Jaccard = intersection/union; tagOverlap (tags intersect — independent of the token set) and pathOverlap booleans; confidence = jaccard plus small additive bonuses for tag/path overlap, clamped to [0,1].
Collect provenance: push 'token-jaccard' when jaccard>0 contributes, 'tag-overlap' when tags intersect, 'shared-path' when paths intersect.
Filter confidence >= 0.6; sort confidence desc then source then target; return.
Add a unit test (doctor-related-graph test) with fixtures covering proposed / not-proposed / already-connected cases.

reference.pattern: Pure factory-free graph helper beside buildRelatedGraph, unit-testable with hand-built fixtures; CJK-aware tokenize for text overlap

rationale.chosen_approach: A pure function mirrors the existing buildRelatedGraph design (I/O separated from logic), so the edge-suggestion heuristic is unit-testable and free of read-set coupling. Reusing tokenize keeps CJK behavior consistent with recall.
decision_factors: purity enables fixture-based tests without a store
reusing tokenize/tag/path primitives avoids a new similarity stack
confidence + provenance give the human reviewer an auditable why
rationale.tradeoffs: Jaccard over summary+intent_clues is a lexical proxy for relatedness (no embeddings); acceptable because the output is advisory and human-gated, and the 0.6 floor keeps proposals sparse.

risks: O(n^2) over the corpus — bounded because it runs only in doctor (not hot path); if corpus is large, the caller in TASK-004 can cap candidates. Note this for TASK-004.
